semantic.role: Defines the syntactic patterns for importing, exporting, and using symbols in PHP source code, enabling ArchSpine to build a dependency graph of the project.
responsibilities: Specify regex-like patterns to detect import statements (use, require, include, etc.)
Specify patterns to detect export declarations (namespace, class, function)
Specify patterns to detect usage of symbols (function calls, method calls, static accesses)
outOfScope: Not a full PHP parser; only covers specific predefined patterns
Does not cover PHP 8+ attributes, anonymous classes, trait use statements, or complex expressions

English.purpose: This document provides the pattern definitions that ArchSpine uses to scan PHP files for import, export, and usage references. It is part of the core scanner configuration and specifies how the mirror system identifies code structure for dependency mapping.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers maintaining the ArchSpine mirror system and AI agents that need to understand how PHP code is analyzed. The patterns are used by the scanner to extract symbols and build a module dependency graph.
key_takeaways: Defines how ArchSpine identifies PHP imports via patterns for `use`, `require`, `require_once`, `include`, `include_once`.
Defines how ArchSpine identifies PHP exports via patterns for `namespace`, `class`, `function` declarations.
Defines how ArchSpine identifies PHP usages via patterns for function calls, method calls, and static accesses.
The patterns use a simple variable template system (e.g., `$SOURCE`, `$NAME`, `$OBJ`, `$ARGS`).
